subtopics.name: Digital Signature Authentication, Authentication Protocols, Digital Signature Standards, RSA Digital Signature, Elgamal based Digital Signature
subtopics.title: Digital Signature Authentication, Authentication Protocols, Digital Signature Standards, RSA Digital Signature, Elgamal based Digital Signature

questions.text:
Person A wants to send a message to Person B. Both agreed to use SHA-1 for obtaining the message digest. Let the generated message digest for the message be 4. Both agree on the public key components {p, q, h} as {23, 11, 7}. Person A selects his private key as 3. Let the pseudorandom integer k be 5. Demonstrate the step-by-step calculation for the following:
• Generate the digital signature using DSS.
• Signature verification.

Given parameters (p, q, h) = (31, 5, 3) and Alice’s private key x = 4; Alice’s random integer k is 3. Hash of message H(M) = 21. Generate a digital signature using the Digital Signature Standard (DSS) and verify the same.

Using the ElGamal digital signature scheme, User A chose p = 13, q = 2, private key X_A = 3, H(m) = 11, k = 5. He announces the global components publicly.
(i) Find the public key Y_A.
(ii) How does User A perform the signing process to compute (S1, S2)?
(iii) How does User B perform the verification process?

Alice wants to sign a message using the ElGamal signature scheme with small values for easy computation. She chooses a prime modulus q = 23, a generator α = 5, and her secret key X_a = 6. She needs to sign a message with hash value h(M) = 9 using a randomly chosen integer k = 7. Illustrate the steps involved in the signing process and the verification process.

Bob wants to generate a digital signature using DSA with the prime modulus p = 67, subgroup order q = 11, and h = 3.
(i) Compute the value of g and check whether it is prime or not.
(ii) Assume Bob selects the per-message secret integer as 7, the pseudorandom integer k as 5, and the hash of the message as 13. What would be the signer’s digital signature components?
(iii) How does the receiver verify the signature components sent by Bob?

Use the RSA digital signature scheme. Given the prime numbers p = 11 and q = 13, and the public key exponent e = 7, perform the following tasks:
(a) How do you know the choice of e is valid?
(b) Compute the private key exponent d.
(c) Generate the digital signature for the given hash code w = 25.
(d) Verify the signature to confirm the integrity and non-repudiation of the source.
Consider the ElGamal signature scheme with p = 467, α = 2, and X_a = 127. Perform detailed signing and verification procedures for the hash of the message h(M) = 100 and k = 213.
Illustrate the signing and verification process of the ElGamal digital signature scheme with a complete step-by-step example.

Given parameters (p, q, h) = (31, 5, 3) and Alice’s private key x = 4; Alice’s random integer k is 3. Hash of message H(M) = 21. Generate a digital signature using the Digital Signature Standard (DSS) and verify the same.

Person A wants to send a message to Person B. Both agreed to use SHA-1 for obtaining the message digest. Let the generated message digest for the message be 4. Both agree on the public key components {p, q, h} as {23, 11, 7}. Person A selects his private key as 3. Let the pseudorandom integer k be 5. Demonstrate the step-by-step calculation for the following:
• Generate the digital signature using DSS.
• Signature verification.

subtopics.name: Authentication Protocols, Kerberos, X.509 Authentication Service
subtopics.title: Authentication Applications: Kerberos, X.509 Authentication Service

questions.text: Describe how Kerberos-V4 provides authentication service in an open distributed environment where users work in different workstations and access services from the servers distributed throughout the network.
Using Kerberos-V4, explain how the authentication service and the ticket-granting service are provided.
Identify three security threats concerning node authentication in a networked environment and, for each threat you have identified, explain the countermeasure used in the Kerberos-V4 protocol.

Describe how Kerberos-V4 provides authentication service in an open distributed environment where users work in different workstations and access services from the servers distributed throughout the network.

subtopics.name: Overview of Embedded Systems, Design challenges, Embedded processor technology, Hardware Design
subtopics.title: Overview of Embedded Systems, Design Challenges, Embedded Processor Technology, Hardware Design

questions.text: 1)Enumerate the design challenges in Embedded Systems
2) Justify why a desktop system could not be defined as an embedded system. Illustrate architecture of PIC microcontroller with sufficient explanation

3)Identify the various features for the following embedded system i. Smart Washing Machine ii. Green House Monitoring iii. Textile Manufacturing Classify the above systems with respect to scalability with necessary justifications
4) Draw the block diagram of an embedded system and compare and contrast Microprocessor and Microcontroller? Explain the architecture of 8051 Microcontroller with neat sketch?
5)compare and contrast desktop systems with an embedded systems and explain the architecture of 8051 microcontroller with neat sketch?
6)How do the different execution modes in ARM architecture optimize system performance, enable efficient multitasking, and ensure robust security in embedded and real-time applications?
1)Explain the features and architecture of 8051 Microcontroller with the help of neat diagram
2)Describe various modes of operations of ARM.

3)State the design challenges and characteristics of an embedded system? Draw the schematic diagram and write an embedded program using 8051 for the following: A door sensor is connected to the P1.1 and buzzer is connected to P1.7. Monitor the door sensor and when it opens, sound the buzzer?
4)write a short note on the following? i. ARM Processor Programmers model ii. PIC microcontroller
5) write a 8051 C program to toggle only P2.5 continuously every 270 msec. Use timer 0, mode 2 to create delay?
6) How do the different execution modes in ARM architecture optimise system performance, enable efficient multitasking and ensure robust security in embedded and real-time operations?
Discuss the working principle of ADC and analyze its importance in an embedded system with a real life scenario
Mention at least three real life examples where digital to analog conversion is required. Design and illustrate R-2R ladder based DAC

What is data acquisition system ? with neat block diagram explain the charecteristics applications and working principle of the following data converters: ADC- Analog to Digital Converter DAC – Digital to Analog Converter
Explain the operation of an R-2R ladder DAC (Digital to Analog converter) in embedded systems when a 3-bit DAC is given an input value of Vref equal to 101 with a neat sketch.

subtopics.name: Timers & Counters, watchdog Timer, UART, Encoders/decoders
subtopics.title: Timers & Counters, Watchdog Timer, UART, Encoders/decoders

questions.text: 1)Design a program to transfer any eight letter word serially at 9600 baud rate, 8 bit data, 1 bit stop and repeat continuously
2)Mention the various use of serial communication. Construct a program using Embedded C/ALP to transfer message “AI-The Devil or Angel” serially with baud rate of 9600 bps using UART protocol.

3) What is watch dog timer? Identify any 5 applications of timers and counters and discuss the working of timers and counters. Write a program for the following? To generate a rectangular waveform of on time 25ms and off time 10mses. Write an 8051 C program to toggle only pin P1.5 continuously every 250msec. Use timer 0, mode2 to create the delay?
4) What is UART? Write a program for 8051 to transfer the data “EXAM” serially at 9600 baud, 8 bit data, and 1 stop bit.
5) Explain the memory interfacing techniques using encoders and decoders

6)What is watch dog timer? Identify any 5 applications of timers and counters and discuss the operation of timers and counters with neat illustration of SFR's and write a program for the following: To generate a rectangular waveform of on time 5ms and off time 15ms and triangular waveform with a delay of 20ms. Assume timer 0, mode 1 with XTAL frequency of 11.059MHZ.

7) Discuss the importance of timers in the 8051 microcontroller and their application in embedded systems and write a code snippet to generate a square wave on PORT 1.0 with a 200ms time period using timer 1 in mode 2 on the 8051 microcontroller users 11.0592 MHz crystal oscillator.
Discuss how actuator could be used to control various types of movements. How such movement control can be applied to design a mobile vacuum cleaner. Show the interfacing and operation with 8051
List any 10 sensors and actuators with their specifications and characteristics. Write the features of PIC microcontroller?

subtopics.name: Evolution of Embedded Programming Tools, Modelling Programs, Code Optimization, Logic Analyzers, Programming Environment
subtopics.title: Evolution of Embedded Programming Tools, Modelling Programs, Code Optimization, Logic Analyzers, Programming Environment

questions.text: List any five hard real-time systems and five soft real-time systems.
Draw the Control Data Flow Graph (CDFG) for an embedded application, namely a fire alarm system.
Illustrate five code optimization techniques, providing relevant examples for each.

Propose a model to capture the behavior of a heartbeat monitoring system designed to continuously monitor a person's pulse and activate a siren alert when the heart rate exceeds a predefined threshold. The system should consist of two main tasks: pulse monitoring and siren activation.

Construct an FSM model for the scenario "Autonomous Vehicle Component Classifier" and also illustrate the CDFG for the system. The system operates in an automotive manufacturing plant with a mobile robot that picks, inspects, and places components. Components must be sorted by type, size, quality, and weight, and collision avoidance must be implemented. Define relevant states, events, and actions to ensure smooth and efficient operation.

Analyze the requirements and design challenges involved in code optimization for embedded system design. Based on your analysis, apply suitable optimization techniques to the provided code snippet and reorganize the code to achieve optimal performance while ensuring that relevant validations are properly incorporated.
What is a logic analyzer? Explain program analysis by drawing the DFG or CDFG for the following control structures, with one example for each: (1) if, (2) while, (3) switch, (4) for.
Identify and explain the various phases involved in translation of a program from source file to hex file for an embedded system with a neat sketch.

Propose a model to capture the behavior of a heartbeat monitoring system that continuously tracks a person's pulse and activates an alert to the doctor's interface when the heart rate exceeds a predefined threshold. The system involves two key tasks: pulse monitoring and notifying the doctor.
Find out the needs for software optimization techniques and analyze various software optimization techniques in detail with respect to embedded system software design using suitable examples.

Construct an FSM model and CDFG for the real-time embedded system "Industrial Temperature Control System" used in industrial environments to maintain temperature within safe operational limits. Requirements include accurate temperature measurement, real-time updates and display, and threshold alarms via audible/visual alerts. Consider relevant states, events, and actions.

Construct an FSM model for the scenario "Pick and Place Robot" used on a factory floor with racks of different sizes, objects supplied via conveyor belt, and a mobile robot with 360° scanning capability. Requirements include optimal rack space utilization and collision avoidance. Illustrate the CDFG for this scenario.
Investigate the challenges and issues faced by embedded system programmers. Using your findings, optimize the following code snippet and provide appropriate justifications:
An autonomous car is programmed to move from VIT to CMC, Bagayam. The car will follow all traffic rules and is guided by inbuilt Google Maps. Construct an FSM and a CDFG for this system.
Make use of various optimization techniques in the given code and present the modified code:

subtopics.name: Classification of Real Time Systems, Issues & Challenges in RTS
subtopics.title: Classification of Real Time Systems, Issues & Challenges in RTS

questions.text: State the design challenges and characteristics of an embedded system. Draw the schematic diagram and write an embedded program using 8051 for the following: A door sensor is connected to P1.1 and a buzzer is connected to P1.7. Monitor the door sensor and when it opens, sound the buzzer.

Identify and evaluate the constraints and challenges in designing any special purpose embedded system and choose the appropriate identified parameters for the following system with enough justifications: GPS (Global Positioning System).

Design and analyze a Real-Time Weather Forecast Station that continuously monitors weather conditions and provides up-to-date forecasts to users such as meteorologists, pilots, and emergency response teams. The system may include sensors for temperature, humidity, barometric pressure, wind speed, and precipitation. Using the given criteria (Types of Events, Functional Correctness, Timeliness), evaluate the system design to ensure it meets the requirements for real-time weather monitoring and forecasting.

Analyze the challenges and issues with respect to embedded system design for the following scenarios:
(i) Logistics
(ii) Home Automation

Identify and evaluate the constraints and challenges in designing any special purpose embedded system and choose the appropriate identified parameters for the following system with enough justifications: ATM (Automated Teller Machine) system.
How do the design requirements and constraints differ between hard and soft real-time systems? Provide one example for each type of system.
Differentiate hardware and software development tools with an example for each in the context of embedded and real-time system design.
"All embedded systems are not real time systems." Justify this statement and give one example of an embedded system that is real time and one that is not.

Identify if the following systems are Soft RTS or Hard RTS with justification:
• A cruise control system
• An ATM machine
• A pacemaker
• A Fitbit watch
• A smart fridge

subtopics.name: Real Time Scheduling Schemes - EDF, RMS & Hybrid Techniques
subtopics.title: Real Time Scheduling Schemes - EDF, RMS & Hybrid Techniques

questions.text: Compare and contrast RMS with EDF. Schedule the given task table using RMS.
Explore and elaborate on the requirements of a real-time hybrid scheduler. Determine the execution of tasks with a detailed task timeline graph for the given tasks.

Using the given dataset, show that RMS creates a feasible schedule. If the current execution times do not allow for a feasible schedule, adjust the execution times, create new values, and try RMS scheduling again. Draw a task timeline graph for at least three cycles.

Evaluate how the following parameters affect the schedulability of real-time tasks: Arrival Time, Current Time / Scheduling point, Execution Time, Rate or Period, Deadline. Suggest an optimal scheduling scheme using any three relevant parameters and apply it for the given dataset. Illustrate the task timeline graph for at least three cycles.

A database system allows multiple clients to read data simultaneously, but only one client can write data at a time. Using POSIX semaphores, implement a solution to: (i) Allow concurrent reads, (ii) Ensure exclusive access for writes, (iii) Prevent starvation of readers or writers. Explain the role of each semaphore and provide implementation with suitable code.
Compare and contrast RMS and EDF. Schedule the given task table using both RMS and EDF.

Evaluate how the following parameters affect the schedulability of real-time tasks: Arrival Time, Current Time / Scheduling point, Execution Time, Rate or Period, Deadline. Suggest an optimal scheduling scheme using any three parameters mentioned above and apply it for the given dataset.

Evaluate how the following parameters affect the schedulability of real-time tasks: Arrival Time, Current Time / Scheduling point, Execution Time, Rate or Period, Deadline. Suggest an optimal scheduling scheme using any three parameters mentioned above and apply it for the given dataset.
Compute the CPU utilization and check whether the given tasks are schedulable in an RTOS. Apply RMS and EDF algorithms on the given data. Illustrate the task timeline graph for two cycles.

A car manufacturing industry has various tasks to be carried out. Task 1 takes 2 seconds and is repeated every 4 seconds, Task 2 takes 3 seconds and is carried out every 8 seconds, and Task 3 takes 4 seconds and is repeated every 24 seconds. Assume all the tasks arrive at time 0. Schedule the tasks based on any two scheduling models and analyse the CPU utilization and the constraints for each model with a time graph.

A smart city project has huge data which is collected and used for prediction and analysis. There are 3 processes which happen simultaneously. Build the 3 processes using POSIX and also incorporate scheduling.

subtopics.name: Data Communications and Networking: A Communications Model – Data Communications Evolution of network, Requirements, Applications
subtopics.title: Data Communications and Networking: A Communications Model – Data Communications, Evolution of Network, Requirements, Applications

takeaways.text: These are basics; mostly direct question can come.

questions.text: You are sending a mail from your VIT mail ID to a professor in Stanford. Identify the network devices (hardware) which are employed in the communication.
Explain the components of a communications model with the help of a suitable diagram.

takeaways.text: Mainly scenario-based questions come from this topic.

questions.text: Suppose you are working as a network administrator in an organization, and a job to redesign the network is assigned to you. You have the following requirements and constraints to design the network: i) The CEO, president, and vice president's computers are connected to each other. In this scenario, choose which topology is best suited and why. ii) The computer centers of the same organization have the requirement that every machine needs to be connected with each other. Sketch the topology for the same.

A defence organization is planning to have the network built for their newly constructed office. There are 3 scientists working on confidential projects. Each scientist has a team of 5 engineers. Sketch a suitable topology with justification.

A trading support centre employs 600 staff members. They have recently expanded and, as a result, need to move to a new building. A building has been identified but has no network. This means that before they move out, new network services need to be designed and implemented in the new building. The new building is expected to have three floors with two departments each, for example, the first floor (Sales and Marketing Department: 120 users expected, HR and Logistics: 120 users expected); the second floor (Finance and Accounts Department: 120 users expected, Admin and Public Relations Department: 120 users expected); and on the third floor (ICT: 120 users expected, Server Room: 12 devices expected). Therefore, as a member of the network design team, you have been asked to design a network for the new building by analysing the number of networking and internetworking devices required per floor. Then suggest the most appropriate topology focusing on scalability, security, and interconnectivity for an individual LAN design and to allow these LANs to connect to public Internet. ii) Show the complete network topology design.

A network uses a fully interconnected mesh topology to connect 10 nodes. Considering full-duplex mode links, calculate the number of total links required and the number of I/O ports required in each node.

On a university campus, there are three departments and each department is going to establish a computer lab. The requirements of each lab are listed below. Your task is to suggest the topology and give justification for selecting the topology in view of cost-effectiveness, reliability, and accessibility. Also, discuss the challenges present in the topology. Department 1: Totally 50 computers have to be connected. Each computer is connected to two others, forming a closed loop. Data has to travel in one direction. Department 2: The lab is situated in a narrow room space, so the network setup has to be with a single central cable running along the length of the lab, with each student's workstation connected to this cable via a short cable. Department 3: This is a research project lab with high-end computers and a printer. The data are so precious that if one device fails or a cable is damaged, the rest of the network should remain unaffected, ensuring uninterrupted service for other users. The financial implications are not a primary concern in this decision-making process.

A company wants to design a network for its new office building that spans four floors. The IT team needs to connect various departments such as finance, HR, and engineering located on different floors. The network must support high-speed internet access, secure communications, and the ability to handle large amounts of data being transferred within the office. The following requirements must be met: a) Each department should have its own dedicated segment to reduce traffic congestion. b) The network should be scalable to accommodate future expansions. c) Security is important, so there must be provisions for isolating departments and preventing unauthorized access to data. d) A wireless network should cover common areas like the lobby and cafeteria. Based on the scenario above, answer the following: i) What type of network topology would you recommend? Justify your choice. ii) Explain how VLANs (Virtual LANs) can help with security and traffic management in this network. iii) How would you implement a wireless network that ensures sufficient coverage and security in common areas? iv) Suggest methods to future-proof this network for scalability.

takeaways.text: Learn different protocols for different layers and be clear about the functionalities of the protocols.

questions.text: Discuss the need for protocols and standards.

takeaways.text: Learn about different layers and their functionalities.
Understand the differences between TCP/IP and OSI models.

questions.text: Application X on computer A sends a message to application Y on computer B. Computers A and B are connected to LAN1 and LAN2, and both LANs are connected to Router R1. Explain the data processing that is performed in every layer with respect to the OSI reference model. Use a diagram to explain your answer.
A company is hiring freshers to work on the Application layer and Transport layer. Identify any 4 protocols with their purpose, which the candidate has to be familiar to be eligible for the job.

Consider two devices which want to communicate with each other. Analyse how TCP/IP model will be used in those two devices with neat sketch. Emphasize the functionalities of each layer and also present the names of encapsulated packets at each layer.

takeaways.text: Very easy topic, learn the diagrams for each switching technique.

questions.text: Differentiate between Circuit switching and datagram packet switching.
Discuss the various phases in Circuit Switching.

Consider an application that transmits data at a constant rate (for example, the sender generates an n-bit unit of data every 'k' time units, where 'k' is small and fixed). Also, when such an application starts, it will continue running for a relatively long period of time. Answer the following questions, briefly justifying your answer: i. Would a packet-switched network or a circuit-switched network be more appropriate for this application? Why? ii. Suppose that a packet-switched network is used and the only traffic in this network comes from such applications as described above. Furthermore, assume that the sum of the application data rates is less than the capacities of each and every link. Is some form of congestion control needed? Why? iii. What advantage does a circuit-switched network have over a packet-switched network?
Enumerate the usage of circuit switching network to connect eight telephones in a small area with a diagram. In addition, analyse the delay and efficiency in circuit switching network.

Consider that the data is packetized before transmission and the endpoint devices do not have any technique to determine if the data is out of order. Which switching will be suitable in this scenario and why? Explain the entire process for this switching using suitable diagrams.
Relevant points substantiate the need for switching techniques in data communication. With a neat diagram, discuss the workings of packet-switching approaches.

subtopics.name: Networking Parameters (Transmission Impairment, Data Rate and Performance)
subtopics.title: Networking Parameters (Transmission Impairment, Data Rate and Performance)

takeaways.text: The problems mentioned in the pdf covers all topics.

questions.text: Evaluate the end-to-end delay (including the transmission delays and propagation delays on each of the three links, but ignoring queuing delays and processing delays) from when the left host begins transmitting the first bit of a packet to the time when the last bit of that packet is received at the server at the right. Link 1: Tranmission Rate: 10Mbps, Link Length: 2 km Link 2: Tranmission Rate: 100Mbps, Link Length: 1000 km Link 3: Tranmission Rate: 100Mbps, Link Length: 3 km The speed of light propagation delay on each link is 2.4x108 m/sec. Note that the transmission rates are in Mbps and the link distances are in Km. Assume a packet length of 10000 bits. Give your answer in milliseconds.

5 equal sized datagrams belonging to the same message travel to the destination one after another. However they travel through different paths as shown in the following table. The delay for each switch (including waiting and processing) is 5, 10, 15, 10 and 20 ms respectively. Assume that the propagation speed is 2*10^8 m/s. Compute the order in which the datagrams arrive at the destination and the delay for each. Ignore any other delays in transmission.
Define Bandwidth and Throughput in terms of Networking. Consider a system generating 20-bit frames and connected through a shared 20kbps channel. Find the throughput if the frame rate is 1000 fps.

30 Megabits of data are sent from a source to destination 10,000 km apart. The bandwidth of the medium is 10Mbps. Propagation speed is 2x10^8 m/s. The processing time of the router is 0.01s. Determine the latency for the first packet. Calculate the Bandwidth-Delay product.
A network with bandwidth of 10 Mbps can pass only an average of 12,000 frames per minute with each frame carrying an average of 10,000 bits. What is the throughput of this network?

What are the propagation time and the transmission time for a 2.5-kbyte message (an e-mail) if the bandwidth of the network is 1 Gbps? Assume that the distance between the sender and the receiver is 12,000 km and that light travels at 3 × 10^8 m/s.

A path in a digital circuit-switched network has a data rate of 1 Gbps. The exchange of 1000 bits is required for the setup and teardown phases. The distance between the two parties is 5000 km. Answer the following questions if the propagation speed is 2 × 10^8 m/s. What is the total delay if 10000 bits of data are exchanged during the data-transfer phase?

Stored on a flash memory device is a 200 megabyte (MB) message to be transmitted by an e-mail from one server to another, passing three nodes of a connectionless network. This network forces packets to be of size 10KB, excluding a packet header of 40 bytes. Nodes are 400 miles apart, and servers are 50 miles away from their corresponding nodes. All transmission links are of type 100Mb/s. The processing time at each node is 0.2 seconds. I. Find the propagation delays per packet between a server and a node and between nodes. II. Find the total time required to send this message.

A circuit-switched network has a setup time of 2 seconds. If the data transmission rate is 64 kbps (kilobits per second) and a user needs to send a 1 MB (megabyte) file, Calculate the total time required to send the file.

In a circuit-switched network, the propagation delay between two nodes is 15 milliseconds, and the setup time is 100 milliseconds. If the data transmission rate is 10 Mbps and the user needs to send 5 MB of data, Calculate the total time required for the communication.

subtopics.name: Error Detection and Correction – Hamming Code, CRC, Checksum
subtopics.title: Error Detection and Correction – Hamming Code, CRC, Checksum

questions.text: A four bit message, 1101 is transmitted through a network with even parity. Now, according to Hamming code policy, examine the following: a) The code-word of the transmitter side. b) If the receiver has received an error code where 5th bit is flipped then how the error is detected and corrected in the receiver side.

A generator polynomial x^5+x^4+x^2+1 is given . Encode the data bit sequence 1010001101 using the generator polynomial and construct the code-word on the transmitter side. Also, evaluate the code at the receiver end and prove that there is no error

In a wireless communication system, a sensor node is transmitting temperature data of 7 bits to a base station. To ensure reliable transmission, Hamming code with even parity is applied to each temperature data. The data received on the receiving end is 01010111101. Identify any error and mention the steps to correct it.
Calculate the minimum hamming distance for the following data strings: 1110, 1011, 1111, 1001, 1001, and 1001. Show the complete calculation.

Suppose the following message has to be transferred to the receiver: "HAVE A GOOD DAY" a) Show the sender checksum calculation and verify and conclude the decision at the receiver side by assuming no error during transmission. [Hint: HEX values for A-Z(0x41-0x59) and empty space 0x20] b) Prove that if one or more bits of a frame are damaged and the corresponding bits or bits of opposite value in a second frame are also damaged, then the sums of those columns will not change, and the receiver will not detect the errors. Assume that the leftmost hex value of frames 1 and 3 is corrupted during transmission.

Apply CRC technique to detect error while transmitting the following message: bits 11001010. Consider the CRC generator as 1010 which is known to both transmitter and receiver. If the MS of code word is received in corrupted manner, then illustrate how the error is detected.

Consider a data word to be transmitted is 10010001. Determine the sent codewords for (i) even parity scheme (ii) odd parity scheme. During transmission, the codeword is modified to 101100111. Determine the Hamming distance between sent and received codewords for (i) even parity scheme (ii) odd parity scheme. Which parity scheme can detect the error? Justify your answer.

The message HELLO WORLD! has to be transferred. At the sender end, determine the internet checksum and the transmitted data. How does the receiver conclude that there is no error in the received message? Show the calculations at the receiver end if there are no errors.
What kind of error is undetectable by the checksum illustrate with an example?

Given the data word 1111011111 and the divisor 11011, i. Show the generation of the code word at the sender site (using binary division) ii. Show the checking of the code word at the receiver site (assume no error).

The source wants to transmit the message M = 11010110001 to the receiver over a noisy channel. Assume the sixth bit is flipped during the transmission. a. Show the code generation on the sender side b. Show how the receiver finds whether the received code word has an error or not. c. What kinds of errors it cannot determine? Why and Justify?

Suppose we want to transmit the message 1011 0010 0100 1011 and protect it from errors using the CRC polynomial x^8+x^2+x+1. i) Use polynomial long division to determine the message that should be transmitted. ii) Suppose the leftmost bit of the message is inverted due to noise on the transmission link. What is the result of the receiver's CRC calculation? How does the receiver know that an error has occurred?

Consider a 16-bit checksum used for error detection. A sender is transmitting the following three 16-bit data words: Data 1: 10011001 11001101. Data 2: 10101010 11110000. Data 3: 11010111 00101101. Calculate the checksum that the sender will append to the data.
A sender wants to transmit the message 110101 using a CRC with a generator polynomial. G(x)=x^3+x+1 (which corresponds to the binary 1011). Find the transmitted codeword.

subtopics.name: Flow control mechanism – Sliding Window Protocol - GoBack - N - Selective Repeat
subtopics.title: Flow Control Mechanism – Sliding Window Protocol: Go-Back-N & Selective Repeat

takeaways.text: Try to do previous year questions; almost similar models come for CAT2.

questions.text: Consider a scenario where Station A needs to transmit a message containing 10 frames to Station B using sliding window protocol. Set the sender's window size as 3. Assume, every 5th transmission is lost, while acknowledgments from the receiver for other transmissions are never lost. After every 5th transmission loss, the remaining frames within the window are sent to the receiver. Subsequently a timeout occurs. Draw the timing diagram and determine the total number of data transmissions made by the sender (including retransmissions) if it follows: i. Go-Back-N protocol ii. Selective Repeat Protocol. Also, calculate the ratio of the effective number of frames sent to the total number of frames sent in each of the above cases.

Consider sliding window with m=2 and demonstrate how Go-Back N protocol will work for the below scenario with a neat time-sequence diagram: Except first packet, all other packets in transmitter window reached the receiver; Acknowledgement for zeroth packet got lost. All other packets and acknowledgements transmitted successfully. Assume there is no loss of packets or acknowledgements during retransmission.

A software developer was given a task to write a code to send 8 frames (assuming every 4th frame is getting lost). He has the option to choose either of the three algorithms, Stop & Wait, Go-Back-3 or Selective Repeat. Justify your answer with the timing diagram.

Draw the sender and receiver windows for a system using Selective Repeat ARQ (3-bits for sequence number), given the following: i) Frame 0 is sent; frame 0 is acknowledged. ii) Frames 1 and 2 are sent; frames 1 and 2 are acknowledged. iii) Frames 3, 4, and 5 are sent; frame 4 is acknowledged; the timer for frame 5 expires. iv) Frames 5, 6, and 7 are sent; frames 4 through 7 are acknowledged.

subtopics.name: Multiple access Aloha - Slotted Aloha
subtopics.title: Multiple Access: Pure Aloha & Slotted Aloha

takeaways.text: Try solving problems on this topic; the problems can be a bit confusing.

questions.text: A slotted ALOHA network transmits 800-bit frames using a shared channel with 400 kbps bandwidth. Find the throughput if the system (all stations together) produces i. 2000 frames per second ii.1000 frames per second iii.500 frames per second. Also find the vulnerability.

Assuming N stations are present in a pure Aloha Network, having a bandwidth of 56Kb/Sec, find the value of N, assuming that each station sends data of size 1000 bits at an average interval of 100 sec.
In a Slotted ALOHA system, the channel has a transmission capacity of 1 Mbps. The average packet size is 1000 bits. Calculate the maximum throughput of the system.

In a Slotted ALOHA network, 10 users are attempting to send packets, and each user generates packets at a rate of 0.5 packets per second. Calculate the probability of a packet collision when one user attempts to send a packet.

Write a detailed comparative analysis of how ALOHA, Slotted ALOHA, CSMA, CSMA/CD, and CSMA/CA react during multiple access transmission in terms of Tfr, Tp, Tb, and vulnerable time. State the reason for Tfr >= 2 x Tp.

Two hosts are in a CSMA/CD network and the medium has a data transfer capacity of 1Gbps. The minimum frame length is fixed to 1,000 bits and the propagation speed is 2 ×10^8 m/s. i) What will be the distance between the hosts? ii) If it is an Ethernet network, what is the efficiency when the hosts have a maximum distance between them? If the distance is reduced to 1 m, does it impact the efficiency?
Distinguish between CSMA/CD and CSMA/CA. With flowchart elucidate the working scenario of CSMA/CA and Pure ALOHA.

In a local area network (LAN) using CSMA, a device called Device A wants to transmit data. Before sending, it listens to the channel and detects that it is idle. Device A begins transmitting its data. Just after it starts transmitting, Device B also senses that the channel is idle and starts transmitting simultaneously. Explain what happens next and how CSMA helps manage this situation.

A network engineer is trying to decide whether to implement a CSMA or CSMA/CD protocol in a newly designed local area network. Discuss the advantages of using CSMA/CD over CSMA in terms of efficiency and collision management.

subtopics.name: IEEE Standards(IEEE802.3 (Ethernet), IEEE802.11(WLAN))- RFID- Bluetooth Standards
subtopics.title: IEEE Standards (802.3 Ethernet, 802.11 WLAN), RFID, Bluetooth

questions.text: X is working as a Business Development Lead in a leading MNC and keeps traveling frequently. X is also given the task of attending online meetings even in transit. An urgent meeting was called for when X was about to board his flight and there was a poor network in his mobile. X contacted the helpdesk requesting for internet connection. Which IEEE standard would help to connect to the airline’s internet service? Enumerate the architecture involved in connecting to the airline’s internet service.

A girl faces an uncomfortable situation while she is travelling in a bus. She immediately alerts her parents using the safety device attached in her mobile phone. Identify the type of connectivity/standards employed in the device with its features.

subtopics.name: IPv4 Address Space – Notations – Classful Addressing – Classless Addressing
subtopics.title: IPv4 Address Space – Notations – Classful Addressing – Classless Addressing

takeaways.text: Practice different model questions from this topic for mastery.

questions.text: An ISP is granted a block of addresses starting with 188.50.0.0/16. The ISP wants to distribute these blocks to 100 customers as follows. a. The first group has 30 medium-size businesses; each needs 128 addresses. b. The second group has 50 small businesses; each needs 64 addresses. c. The third group has 20 households; each needs 32 addresses. Design the subblocks and give the slash notation for each subblock. Find out how many addresses are still available after these allocations.

A network uses the 172.12.0.0 class B address. There is a requirement to support 459 hosts per subnet, while accommodating the maximum number of subnets. Construct the subnet mask and the IP layout for 4 valid subnets with details of the first address, range of address and the broadcast address.

Consider an ISP grants Vellore Institute of Technology, Vellore with a site address as 200.3.2.0/18. The Center for Technical Support department decided to assign the addresses based on the following requirements. Block: SJT, No of addresses: 200; Block: TT, No of addresses: 155; Block: GDN, No of addresses: 120; Block: Pearl, No of addresses: 264; Design the subnets, and find out the available addresses after these allocations. Provide the solution in the table form shown below for each block. (DO NOT CHANGE THE TABLE FORMAT). Addresses must be given in slash notation. SJT BLOCK No. of addresses needed, Network address, Local broadcast address, Prefix length, No. of usable IP addresses, Range of usable IP addresses

An organization is granted with Class A internet protocol and the starting IP address is 101.0.0.0. But the organization wants to divide this network into four equal sub-networks. For each sub-network, tabulate the starting address, ending address, sub-net mask, sub-net number, broadcast address and number of addresses.

A midsized organization is granted 190.168.0.0/16, design a subnetting scheme that supports 8 subnets, with each subnet capable of supporting up to 400 hosts. a) What is the subnet mask? b) What is the range of valid host addresses (Usable addresses), network addresses, and broadcast addresses in each subnet? c) What is the total number of usable IP addresses in this scheme?

A CEO of a company needs privacy in maintaining the number of hosts for his company on the public network with a single IP address. What is the networking technology required for the above and explain the same?

An ISP is granted a block of addresses starting with 150.80.0.0/16. The ISP wants to distribute these blocks to customers as follows: i. The first group has 200 medium-sized businesses, each needing 128 addresses. Find the range of the 200th subnet ii. The second group has 400 small businesses, each needing 16 addresses. Find the range of the 400th subnet iii. The third group has 2048 households, each needing 4 addresses. Find the range of the 2048th subnet. Design the subnets. Find out how many addresses are still available after these allocations.

Assume an organization’s network starting address is 10.10.0.0/16 and divided into equal no. of addresses for 16 blocks. Find the following: i. Class __________ and default Subnet mask for the class ____________ ii. No. of addresses in each subnet works _______________________ iii. Subnet mask of each subnetwork ____________________________ iv. CIDR of each subnetwork ______________________________ v. Write the Starting address and end address of each subnetwork. vi. How many bits are borrowed for subnetwork from host bits?

In a Class B network with the IP address 172.16.0.0 and the default subnet mask, calculate the maximum number of hosts that can be accommodated. i) Calculate the number of subnets and hosts in a /20 subnet. ii) Determine the valid host range for the subnet 172.16.0.0/22. iii) What is the next subnet for 172.16.0.0/24? iv) How many total addresses are in a /19 subnet?
What is NAT? What are the situations where Network Address Translations are required?

subtopics.name: IPv6 Address Structure – IPv4 and IPv6 header format
subtopics.title: IPv6 Address Structure – IPv4 and IPv6 header format

questions.text: Describe the key fields in the IPV4 header and discuss the purpose of each. List the main fields of the IPV6 header.
Discuss the differences between an IPv4 and IPv6 packet header (List any six)

An IPv4 datagram has arrived with the following information in the header(in hexadecimal): 45 00 00 54 00 03 00 00 20 06 00 00 7C 4E 03 02 B4 0E 0F 02
  i. Is the packet fragmented? Justify.
  ii. What is the size of the data?
  iii. How many routers thus the packet travel to? Justify.
  iv. What is the identification number of the packet?
  v. Give the offset value.
  vi. Write the source IP address and Destination IP address.
For the given IPV4 format with values given. 0X4500004da548000080110000c0a82b7fc0a82b01 Analyse if the data is fragmented or corrupted with justification

Consider the router receives the following alignment IPv4 header Ox49200160AB00000005170000503007EEFF001A20
 i) Draw the IPv4 datagram header format and fill the corresponding values by analysing the received datagram. Mention the addresses in dotted decimal representation.
 ii) Find is there any option? if so how bytes of option being carried by this datagram.
 iii) Is this datagram fragmented? if so, check is this the first fragment, middle fragment or last fragment
 iv) Mention the type of data being carried by this datagram.

Consider an IP packet with a length of 4500 bytes that includes a 20-byte IPv4 header and a 40-byte TCP header. The packet is forwarded to an IPV4 router that supports a Maximum Transmission unit MTU of 600 bytes. Assume that the length of the header in all the outgoing fragments of this packet is 20 bytes. Assume that the fragmentation offset value stored in the first fragment is 0. What is the fragmentation offset stored in the third fragment?
Identify the status of flags in IP header format for different scenarios listed below: i) Datagram size=200 bytes and MTU= 400 bytes ii) Datagram size=1500 bytes and MTU= 400 bytes

An IP datagram has arrived with partial information on the header 45000054 00030000 2006…..
 What is the header size
 Are there any options in the packet
 What is the size of the data
 Is the packet fragmented
 How many more routers can the packet travel to?
 What is the protocol number of the payload being carried by the packet?

An IPv4 datagram has arrived as shown below. Answer the following questions based on the received header by drawing the IPv4 Header Format
 1) Are there any options? If yes, give the size of options
 2) Is the packet fragmented? Justify your answer
 3) What is the size of the data?
 4) How many more routers can the packet travel to?
 5) What is the identification number of the packet?

After you finish the router prototype, you must test it with different packet-switching technologies, which may have different MTUs. In a test, your router has 3 links with MTU as below:
 Link 1: 60 bytes, Link 2: 420 bytes, Link 3: 1000 bytes
 i) The router receives a 600-byte IPv4 packet from link 3 and needs to send it to link 2. What will happen? Which header fields need to be updated?
 ii) If the 600-byte IPv4 packet from link 3 has a 20-byte header, at least how many packets need to be sent over link 2? Write down the total length value, flag, and fragmentation offset fields of each outgoing packet.
 iii) The router receives 20 fragmented packets from link 1 and needs to send them to link 3. How many packets will be sent over link 3?

subtopics.name: Routing - Link State and Distance Vector Routing Protocols - Implementation - Performance Analysis - Packet Tracer
subtopics.title: Routing-Link State and Distance Vector Routing Protocols- Implementation-Performance Analysis- Packet Tracer

questions.text: Evaluate the shortest path of all links to A using Dijkstra's Algorithm

Use the Dijkstra's algorithm to solve the shortest path problem for the following graph. Find shortest path from V1 to all other vertices. Draw a table showing the intermediate distance values of all the vertices at each iteration.

The CSE department at Princeton University bought new Sun Fire V210 servers. They decided to run a distance-vector protocol for routing between these servers (even though it is a small network). Apply Bellman Ford Algorithm to construct routing table for node I. Depict all steps. Discuss handling scenario when links between D and E fail.
Apply Bellman Ford Algorithm to construct routing table for node I, assume links between D and E fail. Discuss handling in distance vector scheme.
Apply Link State routing for the network with node 'A' as root. Illustrate Dijkstra's algorithm steps and evaluate shortest path tree and routing table.
Routers 1 to 5 connected, Show count-to-infinity problem if node A fails. Explain solution.
Provide routing tables with knowledge limited to neighbors.
Formulate routing tables using equation D(v)= min(D(v), D(w)+c(w,v)), where D=distance, c=cost, w=source.
Perform Dijkstra algorithm to find shortest paths for nodes 0 and 4.
Distance vector routing in network with 6 routers. Show router 1's tables after 1st and 2nd iterations and final table. How many links are unused and why?
Demonstrate Distance Vector routing algorithm starting from node F, showing steps and shortest paths.
Explain count-to-infinity problem and its solution in distance vector routing. Discuss limitations.
Use link state routing to find shortest paths from node E. Analyse challenges.
Given a company's network of 6 servers with link weights, calculate shortest paths from S1 using Dijkstra, list paths and times.

Discuss routing in multiple autonomous systems: inter-domain routing (BGP) and intra-domain routing (OSPF/RIP). Explain routing loops and their prevention with methods like split horizon and hold-down timers.

In the network with nodes A,B,C,D,E and links as described,A is connected to B and C. B is connected to A, C, and D.C is connected to A, B, and E. D is connected to B. E is connected to C.  analyze adding link B-E with cost 4:
i) Describe LSA flooding and routers' updates.
ii) Calculate new shortest path from D to E.

1) Consider two devices which wants to perform process-to-process communication without establishing a connection. Examine the header format for this type of service and also state its advantages and disadvantages.
1) Differentiate closed loop and open loop congestion control by quoting 2 mechanisms from each method.

2) Consider a leaky bucket used to control liquid flow, how many gallons of liquid are left in the bucket if the output rate is 5 gal/min, there is an input burst of 200 gal/min for 12 s, and there is no input for 48s?

3) Analyse and compare how congestion window size is updated after each RTT in slow start using exponential increase approach and congestion avoidance using additive increase approach with neat sketches.
4) Explain the congestion control techniques used to improve the QoS of the computer network.
5) Explicate various traffic shaping algorithms used by the transport layer in ensuring QoS during communication.
6) Elucidate the role of Queuing Mechanisms in Congestion Control.
7) Deliberate some common Congestion Avoidance Mechanisms used in TCP?
8) Explain with necessary diagrams how Transmission Control Protocol (TCP) uses the congestion control to alleviate congestion in the network.

9) (a) Illustrate the Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) transaction between the client and server and illustrate for the following cases: The client wants to retrieve a document from the server. Use the appropriate method. (i) The client wants to send data to the server. Use the appropriate method. (b) Which transport layer service does HTTP use? Differentiate various connection modes of operation used by HTTP.

subtopics.name: Application Layer, DNS, FTP, HTTP, SMTP, SNMP
subtopics.title: Application Layer, DNS, FTP, HTTP, SMTP, SNMP

questions.text: 1) Client A sends a request to abd.org to get the IP address of vit.edu. Analyse both the Resolution techniques to locate the IP address along with the diagrams.
2) Brief about network management system functionalities. Elaborate on the application layer responsible for rendering the above network management services.

3) Consider a web server like Google and multiple clients which uses HTTP for communication. Analyse the differences between persistent and non-persistent connections with neat diagrams. In addition, present the message format for request from client and response from server.

4) Identify the application layer and transport layer protocols for the following day-to-day networking services: Searching, Online ordering and transactions, Email services, File upload and download.
5) List out the types of DNS messages. With a neat frame format enumerate DNS messages with its header.

6) An international company, "NetCom Corp," provides IT services for several clients. They manage web servers, file servers, email systems, and network devices for clients around the globe. The company uses a variety of protocols to handle different tasks, including FTP for file transfers, HTTP for web services, SMTP for email communication, and SNMP for network monitoring and management. The IT team has been tasked with ensuring smooth operations across all services, and they are facing specific challenges with each protocol.
i) One of the clients reports issues when uploading large files to the company's file server. The transfer seems to take too long, and sometimes it fails mid-way.
a. Explain how FTP works and identify two potential issues that could be causing the slow transfers or failures.
b. What can the IT team do to improve the reliability and speed of file transfers using FTP?
ii) The company's web server, which hosts a critical application, is experiencing high traffic loads, causing some users to report slow page load times.
a. Explain how HTTP functions in the context of web browsing.
b. What techniques can the IT team implement to optimize HTTP performance and reduce the impact of high traffic on the web server?
iii) The IT team notices that some clients are complaining about email delays and failures in sending emails. The emails get stuck in the outbox or fail to reach their destinations.
a. Describe how SMTP handles email delivery and what could cause delays or failures in this protocol.
b. Suggest two strategies the IT team can use to troubleshoot and resolve email delivery issues in their SMTP server.
7) Name the categories of the HTTP header. Write short notes on each header with its descriptions.

subtopics.name: Introduction to OS: Functionality of OS - OS design issues
subtopics.title: Introduction to OS: Functionality of OS & OS Design Issues

questions.text: Assume that you are designing an operating system to run multiple tasks at the same time. Identify the various design issues that need to be addressed.

Considering the diverse architectures of operating systems, analyze the common structural elements they share. How do these commonalities impact the efficiency and functionality of various operating systems, and what might this suggest about the future development of OS design?

subtopics.name: Structuring methods (monolithic, layered, modular, micro-kernel models)
subtopics.title: Structuring Methods: Monolithic, Layered, Modular, Microkernel Models

questions.text: Describe the various methods used for operating system structure. Also discuss the advantages and disadvantages of each structuring method.
Explain Monolithic and Layered system of Operating System Structure.
Compare layered approach and micro-kernel approach of OS structuring models. Discuss the merits and demerits of each approach.

A Computer system can have a single processor, multiprocessor, and multicore processor architecture. A job or program can be executed either sequentially or in parallel based on its design. Enumerate the advantages of having different system architectures and analyze the effect of executing a set of jobs run in sequential or parallel over these architectures.

Evaluate the main advantage of the microkernel approach to system design. Furthermore, analyze how user programs and system services interact within a microkernel architecture. Finally, assess the disadvantages associated with using the microkernel approach.

Evaluate the use of monolithic kernels in real-time scenarios compared to microkernels. In your analysis, discuss specific situations where a monolithic kernel may be preferred and critically assess its advantages over microkernels. Additionally, explore the mechanisms of interaction between user programs and system services in a monolithic architecture, while also considering the potential disadvantages of this approach. How might these factors influence the choice of kernel design in modern operating systems?

subtopics.name: Abstractions, processes, resources - Influence of security, networking, and multimedia
subtopics.title: Abstractions, Processes, Resources - Influence of Security, Networking, and Multimedia

takeaways.text: These are mostly theory concepts—expect direct questions.

questions.text: Elaborate in detail on aspects of security in an OS with respect to various threats.

In a multiprogramming and time-sharing environment, several users share the system simultaneously. This situation can result in various security problems. How to ensure the degree of security in a time-shared machine similar to a dedicated machine? Explain your answer.
The operating system provides tremendous services to the user. From the security perspective, which are the services to be considered? Justify your answer.
Consider a multi-user and multi-programming operating system. Identify the various possible security threats to the resources. How does the OS ensure the security of the system resources?
What is a system call? Why are system calls necessary? Illustrate the methods to pass the parameters of system calls to the OS.
Define system call and system programs and list its categories respectively.

Categorize the following instructions into privileged instructions and non-privileged instructions. Also mention whether the instruction to be executed under user mode or kernel mode. i) Reading system time ii) Clear Memory iii) Opening and reading a file iv) Set the timer v) Performing arithmetic operation
What are the functions of Kernel? Discuss on kernel design and its interface role for user handling with an example
